records.ety: [OE. stalworth, AS. staelwyrð serviceable, probably originally, good at stealing, or worth stealing or taking, and afterwards extended to other causes of estimation. See Steal, v. t., Worth, a.]

defs: Brave; bold; strong; redoubted; daring; vehement; violent.
quotes: Fair man he was and wise, stalworth and bold.
notes: ☞ Stalworth is now disused, or but little used, stalwart having taken its place.
